lead_profile_text: NHS physiotherapist building private clinic. Switching 2-bed Chester city centre property from lodger arrangement to full STL. Friend moves out in ~6 months. Mortgage remortgage due September 2026 — weighing holiday let / BTL company / clear £25k balance. Moving in with partner 2 streets away.
emotional_profile: Deliberate and lifestyle-focused. Wants clean, simple passive income — not a portfolio builder. Appeals to minimum-hassle framing. Professional background creates NHS guest affinity. Measured decision pace.
delay_trigger_phrases: I'll need to speak to my mortgage broker
delay_timeline: September 2026 (mortgage), October/November 2026 (occupier leaves); potential November live date hitting Chester Christmas markets
green_light_trigger_phrases: I want consistent passive income with minimal hassle
I want the flexibility to reduce my hospital days

top_objections.text: What type of guests will the property attract — specifically wants healthcare/NHS workers
top_objections.zac_response: Countess of Chester Hospital is a primary demand driver; NHS guest profile well established in Chester

top_objections.text: Mortgage structure uncertainty — which route to take before committing
top_objections.zac_response: All three options viable; recommend mortgage broker; Stayful works under all structures
Initial premium rate transitions to zero business rates after ~1 year

top_questions.text: What type of guests will I get?
top_questions.answer: NHS/healthcare from Countess of Chester Hospital; racecourse visitors; University of Chester; 400+ annual events
Premium rate initially, transitions to zero business rates after ~1 year

top_questions.text: Which mortgage structure should I use?
top_questions.answer: Holiday let, BTL company, or clear £25k — broker consultation recommended; Stayful works with all

what_moved_them: Chester described as one of most consistent income projections in England
Hospital proximity as NHS guest demand driver — resonated with his professional background
Low-hassle management model — monthly statement and bank transfer
Passive income directly funding private clinic transition
what_fell_flat: No offer — no urgency lever. Engagement pace will be slow until early August

conversion_gaps.gap: Mortgage clarity is the actual gate before commitment
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Push broker conversation in July; August call to confirm decision and structure

conversion_gaps.gap: November live = December Christmas markets — not yet connected in Sam's mind
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Frame September mortgage decision as the unlock to hit Chester Christmas market peak season

offer_framing: No offer made — standard 15% + VAT. No urgency lever available.
zac_close_phrase: Speak early August — mortgage clearer, friend closer to moving, everything aligned for a November start hitting Christmas markets
zac_income_intro: £1,700/month average net — £20,405 gross / £10,109 net annually. Chester demand is year-round and highly consistent.
zac_transition_phrases: The healthcare demand in Chester is year-round — exactly the guest profile you want
The passive income is what funds the private practice transition — simplest version of that plan
zac_reassurance_phrases: I'm not interested in building a portfolio — that's fine, this works perfectly as a single property
Minimal hassle — monthly statement, Slack if needed, quarterly call
